cleavage agent: digested with endoproteinase Lys-C followed by modified trypsin digestion.
collision energy: produced using CID with 35% normalized collision energy.
enrichment method: we carried out immunoprecipitations from HeLa cell cytoplasmic and nuclear extracts.
fractionation method: Analysis of the chromatographic fractions by SDS-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS–PAGE) shows that all three polypeptides co-elute in the same fractions.
fragmentation method: subjected the TAF2–8–10 complex to collision-induced dissociation (CID) experiments.
instrument: All MS experiments were performed on a Quadrupole Time-of-flight (Q-ToF) II mass spectrometer.
ionization type: a nano-LC electrospray ionization source (Thermo Fischer Scientific).
labeling: subjected the immunoprecipitated samples to proteomics analysis by using the multidimensional protein identification technology (MudPIT).
reduction reagent: binding buffer (25 mM HEPES-NaOH, pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT).
reduction concentration: binding buffer (25 mM HEPES-NaOH, pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT).
